title: Retirement Income Drawdown Strategy - Client 042
content: Transitioning from wealth accumulation to retirement income requires careful planning to ensure sustainable distributions while preserving capital for longevity. Client Profile: Client-042, age 65, $4.5M portfolio, $180,000 annual income need (4% withdrawal rate), 30-year planning horizon. No pension, Social Security benefits of $36,000 annually starting at age 70. Income Strategy: Implement systematic withdrawal plan with monthly distributions totaling $15,000 ($180K annually). Asset Allocation: 45% dividend-paying equities for growth and rising income, 40% bonds and fixed income for stability, 10% REITs for inflation protection and income, 5% cash reserves for 6-month liquidity buffer. Bucket Approach: Bucket 1 (Years 1-5): $900K in short-term bonds, CDs, and money market funds ensuring near-term withdrawals from stable sources. Bucket 2 (Years 6-15): $1.8M in balanced portfolio of dividend stocks and intermediate bonds gradually shifting from Bucket 1. Bucket 3 (Years 16-30): $1.8M in growth-oriented equities for long-term appreciation, untouched for minimum 15 years. Tax Optimization: Strategic Roth conversions during lower-income years, qualified dividend treatment for equity income, municipal bonds in taxable accounts. Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) planning starting age 73. Inflation Adjustment: Annual 3% increase in withdrawals maintains purchasing power. Portfolio rebalancing ensures asset allocation targets maintained.
